skill.name: tailwind-design-system
skill.description: Build Tailwind CSS design systems with semantic tokens, reusable component patterns, responsive layouts, dark mode, and accessibility guidance.
skill.summary: Create consistent Tailwind CSS design systems and component libraries.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, browser storage, system reconnaissance, network links, and a critical heuristic combination. Manual review found these are documentation examples and Markdown syntax, with no executable scripts, credential access, or malicious instructions. The only retained concern is low-risk outbound documentation links and example localStorage use for theme preference.

low_findings.title: External documentation links
low_findings.description: The skill includes hardcoded URLs to Tailwind CSS, CVA, shadcn/ui, and Radix documentation. These are reference links, not hidden network requests or data exfiltration paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are in a Resources section and contain ordinary documentation links. No code sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Theme preference stored in localStorage example
low_findings.description: The browser storage access is limited to an example ThemeProvider that reads and writes a configurable theme key. It does not access credentials, tokens, or unrelated browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The localStorage calls are visible in a dark mode implementation example and only persist UI theme preference. There is no network transfer or sensitive key reference.

low_findings.title: False positive command execution and weak crypto detections
low_findings.description: The external command and weak cryptography detections point to Markdown code fences, prose, Tailwind color token examples, CSS variables, and TypeScript snippets. No executable shell command, dynamic evaluation, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context shows documentation and component examples rather than runnable skill code. The static patterns are triggered by backticks, headings, and token names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed critical heuristic combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner combined false-positive code fence detections, documentation links, and local theme preference storage into a critical heuristic. Manual review found no credential collection, obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious execution chain.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant evidence is benign when read in context. No semantic intent to execute commands or exfiltrate data was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent UI patterns that remain flexible across products and screen sizes. This skill provides Tailwind CSS token, component, variant, dark mode, and accessibility patterns for scalable design systems.
actual_capabilities: Defines semantic Tailwind color tokens and CSS variable themes.
Shows CVA-based component variants for buttons, cards, inputs, grids, and dialogs.
Provides responsive layout patterns with reusable Grid and Container components.
Demonstrates dark mode state management with a ThemeProvider and theme toggle.
Includes accessibility guidance for focus states, ARIA attributes, and form errors.
Explains design system best practices for tokens, composition, and theming.
limitations: It is guidance and example code, not an installable component package.
Examples assume React, Tailwind CSS, and related libraries are available.
It does not audit visual designs or verify color contrast automatically.
Teams must adapt tokens and components to their brand and product needs.

use_cases.title: Create a component library
use_cases.description: Build reusable Tailwind components with typed variants, consistent spacing, and predictable states.

use_cases.title: Standardize product UI
use_cases.description: Replace one-off classes with semantic tokens and shared layout patterns across a codebase.

use_cases.title: Add theme support
use_cases.description: Implement light mode, dark mode, and system theme behavior using CSS variables and Tailwind classes.

prompt_templates.title: Start a token system
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Tailwind project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Tailwind design token plan for my app. Include semantic colors, spacing guidance, radius values, and dark mode variables.

prompt_templates.title: Build core components
prompt_templates.prompt: Design Button, Card, Input, and Label components using Tailwind CSS and CVA. Include variants, sizes, focus states, and accessibility notes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor existing UI
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve consistency in an existing codebase.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Tailwind components and propose a design system refactor. Prioritize semantic tokens, shared variants, and reduced class duplication.

prompt_templates.title: Plan advanced theming
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scalable Tailwind theming architecture for light, dark, and brand themes. Include token hierarchy, component contracts, and migration steps.

output_examples.input: Create a button system for a SaaS dashboard.
output: A variant map for primary, secondary, outline, ghost, and destructive buttons.
A size scale for default, small, large, and icon-only buttons.
Accessibility guidance for focus rings, disabled states, and semantic button usage.

output_examples.input: Add dark mode to an existing Tailwind app.
output: A CSS variable token structure for light and dark themes.
A provider pattern for resolving system preference and user selection.
Testing notes for contrast, component states, and persisted theme behavior.

output_examples.input: Make product grids responsive and consistent.
output: Reusable Grid and Container patterns with responsive breakpoints.
Gap and max-width options that align with the design system.
Usage guidance for product cards, dashboards, and content layouts.

best_practices: Use semantic tokens before component-specific tokens to keep themes maintainable.
Define variants in one place so component states remain consistent.
Test components in light mode, dark mode, keyboard navigation, and responsive layouts.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode brand colors directly in every component.
Do not rely on arbitrary Tailwind values when a shared token should exist.
Do not ship components without visible focus states and accessible form feedback.

faq.question: Does this skill install Tailwind CSS for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides design system guidance and examples. You must install and configure project dependencies separately.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written as guidance that can be applied in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Is it only for React projects?
faq.answer: Most examples use React, but the token and Tailwind architecture can inform other frontend frameworks.

faq.question: Does it support dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes CSS variable tokens, class-based dark mode, and a ThemeProvider example.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers focus rings, ARIA attributes, form errors, disabled states, and keyboard-friendly component patterns.

faq.question: Can it replace a full design system audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure implementation, but teams still need design review, contrast checks, and product-specific validation.
